full_text:
Fresh tumor tissue collection, processing and inventory management

- Together with Anastasiya or sometimes alone collect the freshly resected tumor samples from the NKL. Snap freeze and formalin fixation of the tissue pieces at the NKL, bring the remaining tissue to ONCOSYS cell culture. Take the tissue adjecent to the one processed in NKL for enzymatic dissociation. If the tumor is big enough share the remaining piece with Kauppi lab.

For tissue dissociation, organoid culture and FACS staining check the protocol folder https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1smAq533MGUQb8rfZyAnULuoP1kGvQTgr

As well as in data cloud: Färkkilä>data>Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

My hard drive: Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

Transfer the snap frozen tubes from LN2 to respective box in -80. If the normal omentum is collected in 50 ml falcon transfer the tube to -80.

Fix the tissue block over night at room temperature on the shaker located in the lab. Transfer the block to 70 % EtoH and store it in the lab fridge until processing.

I have stored the block in 70 % EtoH upto 2 weeks in the fridge. I wait until there are 3-4 blocks than just processing 1 block! If there are more than 1 block per week they can be processed when they are transferred to 70 % EtoH.

I have been doing the embedding myself with the most flat slide facing the bottom of the mold. It’s OK if you prefer to get it done at the service.

Once the tissue processing and the related experiments are done, update the info on Benchling. Anastasiya will fill in the basic info and transfer the pictures, those doing the processing will fill in the experimental part.

Media preparations

Reagents for the organoid medium are located in +4 (CC room), -20, and -80.

Instructions for preparing the organoid medium is->

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1smAq533MGUQb8rfZyAnULuoP1kGvQTgr

As well as in data cloud: Färkkilä>data>Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

My hard drive: Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

Cytokines are added freshly before using. Better not to prepare more than 100 ml at once, and if the medium is more than 2 months old prepare fresh one.

If any of the reagents need to be ordered or stock solutions need to be prepared details can be found here

Preparation of AdF+++ medium for tissue processing is in the protocol and the reagents are in cell culture fridge.

Extra media bottles are in the cold room.

NB: Use advanced DMEM/F12 for organoid medium and DMEM/F12 for AdF+++ medium.

Fixation of organoid cultures for FFPE and IF analysis

Organoids cultured in 96 well plate or on Shandon slides are fixed on day 3-6 depending on the experiment and how the sample is growing.

Cells from 96 well plate are fixed with 10% formalin (similar to tissues) while the cells on Shandon slides are fixed with 4% PFA. The protocol for these two can be found:

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1smAq533MGUQb8rfZyAnULuoP1kGvQTgr

As well as in data cloud: Färkkilä>data>Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

My hard drive: Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

Upon fixation, Shandon slides can be stored upto couple of months in a moist chamber in the fridge until IF staining.

Organoids from 96 wells are processed as histogel plugs, transferred to cassettes, fixed overnight in 10 % formalin at room temperature and next day transferred to 70 % EtOH, stored in the fridge or processed with other tissue blocks. Detailed protocol is

Organoid plugs are embedded with the flat surface-facing bottom of the mold.

Tissue sectioning HE staining and scanning

I normally process tissue blocks for sectioning and staining when there are 10-15 tumor FFPE blocks. Label super frost plus slides (one slide per block, 4um thick one section per slide) with sample name eg: S060_pOme using solvent resistant marker, and give the slides and blocks to Helena / who is in charge of sectioning.

Since we are not happy with the colors of the HE staining at the facility, we do it in the lab. The protocol for HE staining can be found

Once the HE slides have dried, arrange them on a card board tray, and list the slide names in the second sheet of the GBU scan order form.

List the slide names in the same order as the slides are arranged on a cardboard tray. For eg:

All the scanning orders and the scanned images can be found

As well as in data cloud: Färkkilä>data>Ashwini_Protocols>GBU scanning

My hard drive: Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> GBU scanning

Scanned HE images can be found in the data cloud: https://datacloud.helsinki.fi/index.php/s/ZCSp7Jyz8yG7fcF

My hard drive: HE&Fluorescent images from GBU

When the slides and the order form is ready send the form to bch-gbu@helsinki.fi and bring the slides to 5th floor scanning facility. They will send you an e-mail when the scan is ready and images can be copied to our lab hard drive and later transfer to data cloud and to your computer.

Organoid harvestment for FACS staining or making cell pellet for DNA/RNA isolations

Organoids cultures in 24 well plate are harvested on day 3-6 depending on the experiment and the cell growth. The protocol for dissociating the organoids from omentum gel matrix is here:

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1smAq533MGUQb8rfZyAnULuoP1kGvQTgr

As well as in data cloud: Färkkilä>data>Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

My hard drive: Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

NB: I have tried Organoid Harvesting solution from Cultutex and TriplE both of these do not dissolve the omentum gel completely. For FACS analysis, pasing the organoid pellet + some left out matrix through 100 um strainer helps to get a clear cell suspension. For DNA / RNA isolations, some amount of left over matrix has not been a problem.

Drug treatment optimization

Olaparib +/- Pembrolizumab treatment optimization so far can be found here:

In future, more immunotherapy drugs might need to be tested

FACS staining and data analysis

When the freshly dissociated tumor cells or organoid cultures are ready, take the cell pellet (3 x106 for fresh cells) and 6 wells pooled form 24-well plate for organoids for FACS staining.

We have two FACS antibody panels:

For immune cell profiling and comparison for tumor and matched organoids. All the antibodies in this panel are surface staining so the protocol is short.

For analyzing the olaparib + pembrolizumab treatment response. This includes surface and intra cellular staining and the protocol approx. 3-3.5 hr..

We have all the antibodies for immune cell profiling (1) in the lab fridge. So, the staining can be done in the lab. However, some of the antibodies for drug treatment analysis are not available with us so I do the whole staining at Kerkäläinen’s lab in Haartman.

NB: Antibody locations in Kerkäläinen lab keeps changing so make sure you have all the antibodies day before the staining.

Protocol for FACS staining can be found here:

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1smAq533MGUQb8rfZyAnULuoP1kGvQTgr

As well as in data cloud: Färkkilä>data>Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

My hard drive: Ashwini_Protocols & Data_Protocols> Protocols Färkkilä lab

Important things to note: New compensation needs to be set up every 1-2 months for any antibody panel. Ask Kristen’s help.

Using FlowJO, compensation matrix needs to be adjusted for every compensation along with the sample that is run under that. Ask Kristen’s help.

Once you are happy with the compensation, export EpCAM- DCM- cells from FlowJo into a hard drive, and store it in your computer and also transfer to data cloud. These files are analyzed using CYTO.

All the FACS raw data & compensations can be found in:

My hard drive: Flow results

Tumor and matched organoids FACS data and analysis files

Compensated, EPCAM- DMC-, converted files My hard drive:\Ashwini_Protocols & Data\Flowcytometry\Compensated_DCM-EpCAM-20210203

Data cloud: Färkkilä> data> Ashwini_Protocols & Data\Flowcytometry\Compensated_DCM-EpCAM-2021020

CYTO analysis setting and the data of can be found:

My hard drive:\Ashwini_Protocols & Data\Flowcytometry\Cyto analysis\S038-S060_150221

Data cloud: Färkkilä> data> Ashwini_Protocols & Data\Flowcytometry\ Cyto analysis\S038-S060_150221>with Julia

This folder also contains the power point with detailed workflow/ settings used in CYTO if Matilda or Elina wants to try the same settings.

Manuscript figures and related stuff:

https://datacloud.helsinki.fi/index.php/s/3oDT3yqNHaNBEC4
